Case Studies
01
Mobile App · UX/UI · 2024

MarketMate — How do you make grocery shopping less painful?

A full end-to-end mobile app design for a multi-supermarket grocery platform, built to help UK shoppers compare prices, scan products, and checkout — all in one place.

01 — The Problem

Shoppers were wasting time jumping between apps

UK grocery shoppers frequently use multiple supermarkets — Tesco, Asda, Morrisons, Lidl — to find the best prices. But there was no single app that let them compare, browse, and buy across all stores.

💡
Design Challenge: How might we create a unified grocery experience that feels as simple as shopping at one store, while giving users the power of shopping across many?
02 — Research

Understanding real shopping behaviour

Before opening Figma, I mapped out how people actually shop. Key findings shaped the design:

1
Users want to see prices side-by-side, not navigate between apps
2
Sustainability labelling influences purchasing decisions
3
In-store barcode scanning is a feature users wish existed everywhere
4
Onboarding friction is a major drop-off point
03 — Design Decisions

Every screen had a reason

🏠 Home — Personalised feed
Surfaces deals from stores the user has shopped at before. Reduces cognitive load immediately.
🛒 Personalise — Store selection
Users pick preferred supermarkets during onboarding — they only see what's relevant.
📷 Scan — In-store product lookup
Barcode scanning surfaces price, eco-rating, and checkout option instantly.
💳 Payment — Clean checkout
Designed to feel as trustworthy as a bank app. No clutter, no confusion.
04 — Outcome

A complete, shippable product

MarketMate went from concept to a fully designed, interactive prototype covering the complete user journey across 8+ screens.

8+
Screens designed
5
User flows
5
Supermarkets
What I learned

Designing for multiple data sources taught me how important consistent information architecture is. Sustainability features aren't just nice-to-have — they're a differentiator.

02
Web Design · Redesign · Real Estate · 2024

Meqasa — Redesigning Ghana's #1 property platform

A redesign of Meqasa.com focused on improving visual hierarchy, search experience, and trust signals to make finding a home feel simple.

01 — The Problem

A powerful platform held back by a dated interface

Meqasa had 40,000+ listings but the original site had a dense, cluttered layout that made search feel overwhelming — especially for first-time users already anxious about finding a home.

💡
Design Challenge: How might we modernise Meqasa so that finding a property feels calm, trustworthy, and efficient?
02 — Audit

What the original site was doing wrong

1
Visual hierarchy was flat — everything competed for attention
2
The hero search was undersized — the most important action wasn't prominent
3
Property cards lacked breathing room — listings felt cramped
4
Trust signals were buried — agent counts hidden in footers
03 — Design Decisions

Clarity over complexity

🏔 Hero — Full-bleed imagery
Full-width property photo with large prominent search bar. Emotional impact sets the right tone.
🔍 Search — Tabbed and scannable
For Rent / For Sale / New Units tabs keep navigation visible without cluttering the hero.
🏠 Cards — More space, better images
Enlarged cards with bigger photography. Feels more like a magazine than a classifieds page.
🤝 Trust section
"Work in 3 Easy Steps" addresses user anxiety about property transactions in Ghana.
04 — Outcome

Same content, dramatically clearer experience

The redesign preserved everything Meqasa users rely on while making the experience feel more premium and less overwhelming.

40k+
Listings
3
Flows redesigned
100%
Responsive
What I learned

In high-stakes decisions like buying property, calm and clarity are features. Reducing visual noise is as important as adding new functionality.

About

Designing with purpose, delivering with structure.

I'm Priscilla — a Product Designer and Delivery Lead based in Accra, Ghana. I've spent the last 4 years designing digital products that are intuitive, visually compelling, and actually ship.

At Luupli UK, I sit at the intersection of design and delivery — translating complex requirements into clean user experiences while coordinating the teams that build them. My work spans mobile apps, web platforms, and dashboards across fintech, healthcare, and real estate.

I believe great design solves real problems. I care deeply about the person on the other side of the screen.
